pi_items.source: setup_task.sh: PROJECT_NAME='banking-events' and PROJECT_DIR='/home/ga/IdeaProjects/$PROJECT_NAME' constructs the path
setup_task.sh creates this file at line 50: cat > '$PROJECT_DIR/src/main/java/com/bank/events/TransactionEvent.java'
setup_task.sh creates this file at line 123: cat > '$PROJECT_DIR/src/main/java/com/bank/service/AuditService.java'
setup_task.sh creates this file at line 143: cat > '$PROJECT_DIR/src/test/java/com/bank/events/TransactionEventTest.java'

privileged_info_summary: The task involves refactoring a Java POJO (TransactionEvent) to a Java Record. The project is created synthetically by setup_task.sh in the directory /home/ga/IdeaProjects/banking-events. The target file (TransactionEvent.java) initially contains a traditional POJO with explicit getters (getId, getAmount, getCurrency, getTimestamp), explicit implementations of equals(), hashCode(), and toString() methods, and constructor validation logic that ensures amount > 0. The AuditService.java file initially uses POJO getters (event.getAmount()) that must be converted to record accessors (event.amount()). The test file TransactionEventTest.java initially uses explicit getters. The project uses Java 17, Maven 4.0.0, and JUnit Jupiter 5.9.2 for testing.
